item.title: US Investors Back Brazil’s Pax in Bet on AI-Powered Policing
item.description: A Brazilian artificial intelligence startup that helps police investigate violent crime is planning a rapid expansion at home, betting its technology is ready for wider use as Brazil confronts mounting public security concerns.

item.title: Pimco Says Junk Debt for Data Centers Diverging Into Two Markets
item.description: Pacific Investment Management Co.’s leveraged finance chief urged investors to be cautious with the high-yield debt that finances data centers, where winners and losers are starting to emerge as issuance booms.

item.title: Jefferies Sued by Fund Investors Over ‘Massive’ Water Bond Fraud
item.description: Jefferies Financial Group Inc. has been hit with an investor class action lawsuit over bond purchases made by its 352 Capital fund in a water vending machine firm that federal prosecutors have called a scam.

item.title: FTC Chairman Ferguson Says Agency Probing Fertilizer Industry
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission recently launched an antitrust investigation into the rising costs of fertilizer in the US, the agency’s head said at an event in Texas on Thursday.

item.title: Anthropic Raises at $965 Billion Valuation, Eclipsing OpenAI
item.description: Anthropic PBC raised $65 billion in a funding round that valued the artificial intelligence company at $965 billion including the new investment, eclipsing rival OpenAI’s value for the first time.

item.title: Brazil Job Creation Undershoots All Analyst Forecasts in April
item.description: Brazil’s formal job creation slowed much more than expected in April following a surprise surge the month before, as investors mull how much room central bankers have to cut their interest rate.

item.title: Union Pacific, Norfolk Tumble as Regulator Pauses Merger Review
item.description: Shares of Union Pacific Corp. and Norfolk Southern Corp. slumped the most in more than a year after a key regulator paused its review of their planned $72 billion merger, potentially delaying what would be the biggest rail deal ever.

item.title: Three Exchange-Traded Funds to Watch
item.description: From leveraged bets on obscure semiconductor firms to thematic products targeting nicotine, check out these latest—and in some cases curious—launches.

item.title: South Africa Monthly Factory Inflation Hits Record on War Shock
item.description: South Africa’s monthly producer inflation accelerated to a record in April, as higher fertilizer and energy costs tied to the Iran war pushed up prices.

item.title: Why Prediction Markets Are a New Frontier for Insider Trading
item.description: Prediction markets, where users can place bets on real-world outcomes in anything from sports and award shows to elections, interest rate decisions, and even military operations, have become a multi-billion-dollar industry in just a few years. Along the way, they have also become the focus of growing concern that some participants are using privileged information to profit.

item.title: Drugmaker Apotex Files for Canada IPO Showing Revenue Jump
item.description: Canadian generic drugmaker Apotex Health Corp. filed for an initial public offering in Toronto, it said in a statement on Thursday.

item.title: BYD Debuts China’s Most Advanced EV Chip in Smart-Driving Push
item.description: BYD Co., the world’s largest electric vehicle maker, unveiled a series of technology advances, including what it calls China’s first automotive-grade 4-nanometer chip for self-driving cars.

item.title: Anthropic Unveils New Flagship AI Model That’s Better at Coding
item.description: Anthropic PBC is rolling out an updated version of its most powerful artificial intelligence model barely a month after the prior release, a bid to keep ahead of rivals like OpenAI and Alphabet Inc.’s Google.

item.title: Citadel Securities’ Rubner Says Stocks Pain Trade Points Higher
item.description: The “pain trade” in US equities is for prices to keep rising even after a double-digit surge over the past two months, according to Citadel Securities’ Scott Rubner.

item.title: EU Says It Needs €120 Billion to Revive Local Chip Production
item.description: The European Union is making a fresh attempt to revive its faltering semiconductor industry via a rebooted Chips Act, an effort expected to require €120 billion ($140 billion) in public-private investment by 2035, according to draft plans seen by Bloomberg News.

item.title: US Defense Orders Surge to Near-Record Amid War With Iran
item.description: US orders for defense capital goods surged in April to the second-highest level on record, adding to signs that the Iran war is boosting demand for military hardware.

item.title: EU Braces for Talks With Anthropic on Access to Mythos AI Model
item.description: European Commission officials are planning to meet with Anthropic PBC to seek more information on the company’s Mythos model and request making the groundbreaking artificial intelligence tool available to the bloc, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Microsoft Partner IREN Borrows $3.6 Billion for Nvidia Chips
item.description: IREN Ltd., a company that builds and operates data centers for training artificial intelligence models, borrowed about $3.6 billion to provide computing capacity to Microsoft Corp.

item.title: Petrobras Hikes Gasoline Prices After Brazil Subsidies Approved
item.description: Brazil’s oil producer Petrobras is increasing domestic gasoline prices for distributors after the government approved new subsidies to shield consumers from Iran war-fueled crude price hikes.

item.title: Carney Pitches US on Closer Ties in Autos, Aluminum and Minerals
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney used a speech in New York to make the case to the Trump administration for closer cooperation on aluminum, auto manufacturing and critical minerals.

item.title: US Mortgage Rates Tick Up to 6.53%, Freddie Mac Says
item.description: The average rate for 30-year fixed loans was up slightly after biggest weekly jump since March.

item.title: US Stocks Reverse Losses on Iran Deal Report as Oil Pares Gains
item.description: Stocks rebounded from earlier losses on Thursday, as investors reacted to a reported deal between the US and Iran.

item.title: Citadel Securities Sees Markets Underpricing Signs of Iran Deal
item.description: Investors may be underestimating the chances of a “timely” reopening of the Strait of Hormuz triggering a broad relief rally across financial markets, according to Citadel Securities.

item.title: Copasa Relaunches Share Offering After Bids Came Below Minimum
item.description: Cia de Saneamento de Minas Gerais announced new terms for its privatization process, after two bids for a 30% stake in the Brazilian water utility came in below the government’s expectations.

item.title: Dollar Falls Against Major Peers on Report of US-Iran Deal
item.description: The dollar fell against all of its major peers after a report that the US and Iran reached an agreement to extend a ceasefire, pending the approval of President Donald Trump.

item.title: Private Credit Snubs $2.5 Billion Deal by Thoma Bravo’s Sophos
item.description: Just last year, private credit firms were falling over themselves to lend to software borrowers like Sophos. Now, AI anxiety has snarled up the market so much that Thoma Bravo’s $2.5 billion refinancing for the cybersecurity firm is facing hesitant lenders.

item.title: Delfin Is Close to Proceeding With First US Floating LNG Project
item.description: Delfin Midstream Inc. is close to greenlighting what would be the first floating liquefied natural gas export site in the US, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Waymo to Deploy Robotaxi Built With Zeekr to Expand Public Rides
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Waymo unit will deploy a new autonomous vehicle designed specifically for robotaxi use, offering public rides in California and Arizona without human supervision.

item.title: Brazil Central Bank Director Sounds Alarm on Inflation Outlook
item.description: Brazil central bank Monetary Policy Director Nilton David said rising 2028 inflation expectations are a growing concern for policymakers, highlighting fears that a global energy shock could pressure the long-term outlook.

item.title: Avis’ Shock Split With Vendor Revives Credit’s Big Software Fear
item.description: It in many ways embodies investors’ worst fears of how a SaaSpocalypse might play out.
